## Prabu Karthick Parameshwar

<LOCATION>, <LOCATION>, <LOCATION>

<EMAIL>                                                                Phone: <PHONE>

## SUMMARY

-  Highly-driven  researcher  with  7+  years  of  experience  in cell  culture,  biomaterials,  microfabrication  and tissue engineering as well as strong leadership and cross-disciplinary research expertise
-  Looking to join a biotech company to contribute towards research efforts (willing to relocate)

## EDUCATION

- o Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.), Biological and Biomedical Engineering (cGPA: 3.95/4.00)    2017-2024

Faculty of Medicine and Health Sciences, McGill University, <LOCATION>, <LOCATION>, <LOCATION>

-  Fast-tracked from Master of Engineering (M. Eng - Thesis Based) in 2019
-  Thesis: Microenvironmental regulation of placental trophoblast fusion
- o Bachelor of Applied Science (B.A.Sc.) in Engineering Science

(cGPA: 3.27/4.00)    2013-2017

Faculty of Applied Science and Engineering, University of Toronto, <LOCATION>, <LOCATION>, <LOCATION>

-  Major: Biomedical Systems Engineering
-  Thesis: Mechanical stimulation of cell-seeded hydrogels for connective tissue engineering.

## SKILLS & EXPERTISE

- Technical Skills: human cell culture (cell lines, primary cells, stem cells, co-culture), differentiation, histology, microscopy (fluorescence, confocal), microfabrication, microfluidics, instrumentation, 3D (bio)printing
- Bioassays: RNA/DNA extraction, PCR, gel electrophoresis, UV spectrometry, immunostaining, ELISA
- Software: MS Office, AutoCAD, LabVIEW, ImageJ, <LOCATION>, MATLAB, G-code, Statistics - JASP, JMP
- Lab Certifications: Biosafety, WHMIS 2015, Hazardous waste management, up-to-date vaccinations
- Soft skills : Organization, time management, critical thinking, resilience and adaptability

## RESEARCH EXPERIENCE

- Graduate Research Scientist @ McGill University

Sept 2017-April 2024

- o Worked with Dr.  <CANDIDATE_NAME> on in vitro differentiation of placenta trophoblast cells to create fused sheets through biomechanical and biomaterial interventional methods
- o Designed and implemented a process to fabricate nanoparticles from decellularized placentas, as well as characterized its efficacy in using it as a fusion-inducing biomaterial coating on hydrogels with cell sheets
- o Collaborated with Dr. <CANDIDATE_NAME> at the Montréal General Hospital to evaluate mechanical compression protocols and enhanced pro-fusion conditions by up to 30%  in 2D cell sheets/3D spheroids
- o Modified an existing 3D printer to create a moving UV light source for lithography applications
- o Developed and established an SOP for the fabrication and utilization of a microfluidic device for extended culture of testes tissues over 14 days to assess spermatogenesis in a rat model
-  Done in collaboration with Dr.  <CANDIDATE_NAME> at  the Research Institute of  the  McGill University Health Centre (RI-MUHC) from 2019-2022; eventually to be used for human experiments
- o Lead the planning, experimental design and execution of 7 multidisciplinary projects along with supervising 5 undergraduate research students, resulting in 5 conference presentations, 4 journal articles, 1 technical note and 2 literature review publications (Google Scholar)
- Undergraduate Research Scientist @ University of Toronto

May 2016-April 2017

- o Worked  with Dr.  <CANDIDATE_NAME> to  implement  high-frequency  mechanical  stimulation  on  hydrogelencapsulated mesenchymal stem cells and maximally promoted differentiation into myofibroblasts and their protein production over 7 days
- o Created a basic experimental setup and developed a computer interface to perform electrical cell impedance sensing (ECIS) over several weeks in a blood-brain barrier model

-  Lead laboratory sessions, demonstrated experiments and assessed lab reports; 180 hours per term
-  Delivered a guest lecture on MATLAB for simulation analysis (2021-2022)
-  Designed a new lab module on using Design of Experiments (DoE) for bead size analysis (2023)
-  BIEN 530 (Imaging and Bioanalytical Instrumentation)

Jan-Apr 2021

-  Graduate/Undergraduate level course which provides an overview of several microscopy systems and image analysis techniques using ImageJ (30 students)
-  Assessed problem sets, midterms and partially graded final projects; 85 hours per term

## EXTRA-CURRICULARS

- Graduate Orientation Ambassador @
- Campus Life & Engagement, McGill University 2018-2023

pitch_text:
## Document 2: Assignment 4 (Pitch)

Selected Job Offer Position: R&D Scientist, Tissue Therapeutics Target: Aspect Biosystems or BlueRock Therapeutics Key Requirements:

- PhD in Biomedical Engineering or related field.
- Experience with 3D bioprinting, microfluidics, and cell culture.
- Hands-on experience with equipment maintenance and modification.
- Knowledge of GMP and Design of Experiments (DoE).

## The Elevator Pitch

"Hi, I'm <CANDIDATE_NAME>. I am a Biomedical Engineer who builds the tools that make tissue engineering possible .

During my PhD at <CANDIDATE_NAME>, I didn't just use commercial equipment; I modified 3D printers to create custom lithography systems and built a microfabrication cleanroom from the ground up. I used these tools to engineer complex placental and testicular tissue models, keeping cells alive and functional for weeks in microfluidic devices I designed myself.

I know that in a company like yours, you need people who can troubleshoot the hardware and the biology. Because I've managed a cleanroom facility and trained over 30 users, I bring a unique 'process mindset' to R&D. I've recently deepened this with training in GMP and Design of Experiments (DoE) to ensure my innovations are scalable.

I'm looking to bring this blend of engineering grit and biological expertise to your team to help advance your next generation of cell therapies."

clarify_text.ikigai:
My Ikigai is building the infrastructure for biological discovery .

The central theme of my career is "Enabling Innovation." I don't just study biology; I engineer the environments where it thrives-whether that's designing a microfluidic device to keep rat testes tissue alive for 14 days or physically building a cleanroom from scratch at <CANDIDATE_NAME>. My talent lies in this intersection of wet-lab biology (placental cells, stem cells) and hard engineering (microfabrication, 3D printing).

The world needs robust, scalable platforms for tissue engineering and regenerative medicine. By combining my hands-on "maker" mindset with my rigorous scientific training, I can create a lasting impact: developing the tools and processes that turn fragile cells into functional therapies.

To align my career with this, I am actively targeting Biotech R&D and Process Engineering roles. I am ready to apply the resilience I developed during my personal challenges to the fast-paced, problem-solving environment of industry, moving from "academic publication" to "product realization."
clarify_text.ideal_day:
My ideal day is a dynamic mix of hands-on fabrication and experimental execution .

It starts in the cleanroom (or a similar controlled environment), where I am prototyping a new microfluidic chip design or calibrating a 3D bioprinter. I love the focus required to tweak a G-code script or align a UV light source-skills I honed by modifying equipment during my PhD.

By mid-day, I transition to the tissue culture hood. I am seeding cells into the device I just built, testing a new biomaterial coating derived from decellularized tissue. The afternoon involves data analysis-using MATLAB or JMP to statistically validate the results of a Design of Experiments (DoE) run. The day ends with a team huddle, where I might troubleshoot a colleague's experimental setup, leveraging my experience as a Cleanroom Manager to ensure everyone is working safely and efficiently.
clarify_text.smart_goals:
- Goal 1 (Career Placement): Secure a Scientist or Engineer role in a Canadian biotech company (Target: Aspect Biosystems, BlueRock, or STEMCELL) by July 2026.
- Measurable: Apply to 5 roles weekly; network with 2 employees from my target list per month.
- Attainable: I have already had interviews with similar companies; I need to refine my closing interview performance.
- Goal 2 (Skill Certification): Complete the "Regulatory Affairs for Biologics" certification to solidify my knowledge of GMP/CMC by May 2026.
- Measurable: Certificate obtained.
- Realistic: Builds on the Cytiva courses I completed in 2024/2025.
- Goal 3 (Professional Networking): Re-engage with the scientific community after my personal gap by attending 2 local biotech networking events (virtual or in-person) in the <LOCATION> or <LOCATION> area by August 2026.
- Measurable: attendance and follow-up with 3 new contacts per event.
- Time-bound: Next 6 months.
- <LOCATION>

Yes, 2 so far
1. Biomaterials Engineer: passed screening interview with recruiter but did not meet client and was given vague reasons such as being busy, funding issues and eventually just ghosted...
2. Scientist - Biomaterials and Product Assembly Development: went through 2 rounds of interviews (screening and technical) but was not selected, no reason or feedback was provided
